behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the file path, class name (NemotronHConfig), the four deprecated parameter names to remove, the four new parameter names to add with their defaults, and mentions the check_docstrings/check-repo context. All test-asserted specifics (parameter names, defaults like n_groups=8, expand=2, use_conv_bias=True, chunk_size=128, existing params like mamba_hidden_act, etc.) are discoverable from the instruction or from inspecting the file as directed.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes what is broken (docstring documents wrong parameter names) and what to fix (replace four deprecated names with four actual attribute names), but does not give exact line numbers, exact replacement text, or the precise patch. The agent must still inspect the file and craft the correct docstring entries.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check that (1) documented params match class annotations, (2) four new params appear in docstring, (3) four old params are removed, (4) existing params preserved, (5) annotations have correct defaults, (6) formatting passes. These are behavioral checks - any correct docstring rewrite with different wording/formatting would pass, as tests use regex to extract param names rather than checking exact text.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The core fail-to-pass tests (test_docstring_params_match_class_annotations, test_new_mamba_params_documented, test_old_deprecated_names_removed) all work by regex-matching text in the docstring via _docstring_param_names() which does `re.findall(r'^\s{4}(\w+)\s*\(', ...)`. They never import or call NemotronHConfig. While some tests run subprocess linters (ruff), the primary behavioral tests are purely text-inspection of the source file, not execution of the code.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests cannot be satisfied without actually modifying the docstring: test_old_deprecated_names_removed will fail on the original file (which has mamba_dt_min etc.), test_new_mamba_params_documented will fail without adding n_groups/expand/use_conv_bias/chunk_size to the docstring, and test_docstring_params_match_class_annotations checks all documented params against annotations. A stub/no-op won't pass.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/transformers (accessible in container) as solve.sh with the exact patch. The agent could read /solution/solve.sh or the test file at tests/test_outputs.py to extract the exact expected parameter names and their required format. The Dockerfile does not remove or protect these files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors from the instruction are tested: removing 4 deprecated names (test_old_deprecated_names_removed), adding 4 new params (test_new_mamba_params_documented), matching annotations (test_docstring_params_match_class_annotations), preserving existing params (test_existing_params_preserved), keeping backward compat aliases in __post_init__ (test_backward_compat_aliases_in_post_init), and annotation defaults (test_annotation_defaults_consistent).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived, Repo tests), descriptive function names, and docstrings explaining each test's purpose and mapping to check categories.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves editing a Python docstring, not producing structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: No typos found in filenames, paths, commands, or variable names in the instruction. (Note: the gold patch has typos like 'Whetehr' and 'CHunk' but these are in the solution, not the instruction.)

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the specific file, which is appropriate since it's a single-file docstring fix task. It doesn't give line numbers or exact replacement text, just identifies the parameter names involved, which is the domain knowledge needed to understand the bug.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task (editing a Python source file's docstring), not an agentmd-edits/config task.
